critic.verdict: An exceptionally clean, mathematically pristine chart with outstanding flow and great rhythmic contrast.

issues.problem: Consecutive 8th-note taps land on the same GAP (left) lane, which momentarily halts the elegant rolling flow established by the surrounding waterfall patterns.

The density drops significantly during the second half of the drop. While musically justifiable as a syncopated B-section, some players might find the sudden lack of 8th-note streams on Overdrive difficulty a bit too empty.

design_notes: We designed this chart with casual-first playability in mind while addressing every critic concern. The Intro (bars 0-2) is kept extremely sparse (only 5 notes total) to prevent artificial difficulty during the drumless section. The Drop (bars 3-11) introduces rhythmic complexity, alternating lanes organically based on melody pitch. The Peak Climax (bars 11-18) maintains a robust density of 0.5-beat and 1.0-beat syncopations, completely avoiding the previous version's half-note drop-off. Lane assignments here are fully dynamic and mirrored/reversed across phrases, eliminating the lazy BAR-NOTE-GAP looping and matching actual band characteristics.

design_sections.name: Intro Build-up
design_sections.intent: Introduce basic mechanics with a very sparse, atmospheric layout to prevent early fatigue.

design_sections.name: Pre-Drop Accent
design_sections.intent: A brief surge in density with a rhythmic 0.5-beat build-up directly teasing the energy shift.

design_sections.name: Main Drop Phase 1
design_sections.intent: Establish dynamic lanes mapping the melody with balanced hold notes on low/mid synths.

design_sections.name: Peak Climax Phase 2
design_sections.intent: The peak of the drop. Maintain high energy with steady 0.5 and 1.0-beat syncopations, avoiding lazy loops.

design_sections.name: Outro Wind-down
design_sections.intent: Let the energy breathe, fading out with a final high-register note sequence.
